title: Metadata manager for tracking folders and item properties with persistence
purpose: Manages folder structure and item metadata including locking, deprecation, pinning, and blueprint status, with file-based persistence.
module_context: Exports a MetadataManager class that handles loading, saving, and updating metadata in a JSON file.

steps.content: Check if the metadata file exists in the session directory.
IF the file exists, read and parse its contents into the metadata object.
IF parsing fails, log the error and initialize with default empty metadata.
IF the file does not exist, initialize with default empty metadata.
Serialize the current metadata object to a JSON string with indentation.
Write the serialized metadata to the metadata file.
Mark the metadata as no longer dirty after successful write.
Validate that the target folder exists if it is not null.
Initialize item metadata if it does not already exist.
Set the folder property of the item metadata to the provided folder.
Persist the updated metadata to the file system.
Initialize item metadata if it does not already exist.
Set the locked property of the item metadata to the provided value.
Persist the updated metadata to the file system.
Initialize item metadata if it does not already exist.
IF a folder update is provided, validate the folder exists and update the item's folder.
IF a locked update is provided, update the item's locked status.
IF a deprecated update is provided, update the item's deprecated status.
IF a pinned update is provided, update the item's pinned status.
IF a blueprint update is provided, update the item's blueprint status.
Persist the updated metadata to the file system.
Validate that the folder name does not already exist.
Add the new folder name to the list of folders.
Persist the updated metadata to the file system.
Find the index of the old folder name in the folders list.
IF the old folder does not exist, throw an error.
Validate that the new folder name does not already exist.
Replace the old folder name with the new folder name in the folders list.
Update all items that were in the old folder to be in the new folder.
Persist the updated metadata to the file system.
Find the index of the folder name in the folders list.
IF the folder does not exist, throw an error.
Check if items exist in the folder and if items should not be moved to root.
IF items exist and should not be moved, throw an error.
Move all items in the folder to the root folder.
Remove the folder from the folders list.
Persist the updated metadata to the file system.
Check if the item exists in the metadata.
IF the item exists, delete it from the metadata.
Persist the updated metadata to the file system.
